reasoning: Zohran Mamdani is the Democratic nominee for the 2025 NYC mayoral election and is the heavy favorite to win the election. According to multiple late October 2025 polls and prediction markets, his chance of winning is estimated to be around 90-93%. His main opponents are Andrew Cuomo (running as an Independent) and Curtis Sliwa (Republican). Since Mamdani is practically guaranteed to finish either 1st or 2nd (Sliwa is trailing far behind), the probability of Mamdani placing exactly second is almost exactly the probability of him losing the election to Cuomo. Given his wide, double-digit lead in the polls (e.g. 52% to 28% or 43% to 29%) and the fact that early voting has already started with Sliwa still on the ballot splitting the anti-Mamdani vote, Cuomo's chances of an upset are slim. Prediction markets and betting odds consistently put Cuomo's chances of winning around 7-10%. Therefore, the probability that Mamdani secures second place is estimated at around 9%.
